steps.title: Welcome to Day 13: AI for Growth & Analytics
steps.body:
Growth without data is just guessing. Analytics without action is just watching.

Today you'll learn how AI transforms growth analytics — from understanding what's happening to knowing why it's happening to predicting what will happen next.

You'll learn:
• How AI-powered analytics tools uncover insights humans miss
• How to automate A/B testing analysis and get clear winners faster
• How cohort analysis with AI reveals user behavior patterns
• How funnel optimization works — finding and fixing drop-off points
• How predictive analytics forecasts growth before it happens

By the end of today, you'll be able to build an AI-powered growth analytics system that turns raw data into actionable growth strategies — on autopilot.

Here's how AI transforms growth analytics:

📊 DESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS — What happened? AI scans dashboards, finds trends, anomalies, and correlations that humans scroll past. It never gets chart-blind.

🔍 DIAGNOSTIC ANALYTICS — Why did it happen? AI connects dots across data sources — marketing spend → traffic → conversions → revenue — and surfaces root causes automatically.

🧪 PREDICTIVE ANALYTICS — What will happen? AI forecasts metrics based on historical patterns, seasonality, and current trends. See the dip before it arrives.

💡 PRESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS — What should we do? AI recommends specific actions based on what the data says. Not just insights — decisions.

🎯 GROWTH AUTOMATION — AI acts on insights automatically: adjust bids, reallocate budget, trigger email sequences, optimize content. Insights become actions without you touching a dashboard.
highlights: DIAGNOSTIC ANALYTICS — Why did it happen?
PRESCRIPTIVE ANALYTICS — What should we do?

steps.question: What's the biggest advantage AI brings to analytics compared to traditional dashboard tools?
options: AI dashboards look better than traditional ones
AI can analyze complex patterns, correlations, and trends across multiple data sources at once — surfacing insights a human analyst might miss or never have time to investigate
AI replaces the need for any human data interpretation
AI analytics tools are always free
feedback: Exactly. The volume of data modern businesses generate far exceeds what any human can analyze manually. AI doesn't replace the analyst — it amplifies them by doing the pattern-finding and correlation-spotting that would take a person days or weeks.

Good thought, but AI doesn't replace human judgment in analytics. It accelerates the pattern recognition and data processing so the human analyst can focus on interpretation and action. Speed + judgment = the operator advantage.

steps.question: AI-powered analytics is only useful for large companies with millions of data points — small businesses don't generate enough data for AI to find meaningful patterns.
feedback: Correct. AI analytics works at any scale. Even with small data sets, AI can identify trends, anomalies, and correlations that matter. The key is asking the right questions — not the volume of data. A local boutique with 200 customers can still find segments, repeat purchase patterns, and churn signals with AI.

Not true at all. Small data sets still contain patterns — seasonal trends, customer segments, repeat behaviors. AI finds them where a manual review of a spreadsheet might miss them. The technique works at any scale.

steps.title: Priya's AI Growth Engine
steps.body:
Priya runs a DTC subscription box company. She was spending 15 hours per week on analytics — pulling reports, staring at dashboards, trying to figure out why churn was rising.

Her AI-powered growth analytics system:

1. DATA CONNECTION — AI connects to Stripe, Google Analytics, Shopify, and email platform
2. AUTOMATED ANALYSIS — Every Monday, AI generates a growth snapshot:
   • Revenue trends vs forecast
   • Cohort retention rates (who stays, who leaves)
   • Funnel conversion rates (visit → cart → purchase)
   • Anomaly detection (unusual spikes or drops)
3. ROOT CAUSE — When churn increased by 15%, AI cross-referenced: new customers from Facebook Ads had 40% lower retention than organic customers. The audience targeting was wrong.
4. RECOMMENDATION — AI suggested: shift Facebook budget to retargeting existing customers (higher LTV) and refine audience criteria for acquisition campaigns.

Result: Priya went from 15 hours to 2 hours on analytics. Churn dropped 22% in 6 weeks. Revenue grew 18%.

steps.question: Setting up an AI system that monitors your weekly revenue, customer acquisition cost, and churn rate — automatically flagging anomalies and suggesting root causes.
feedback: This is AI analytics at its best. Repetitive monitoring, pattern recognition across multiple metrics, and automated diagnosis. You provide the business context; AI provides the signal detection at scale. This saves hours every week while catching things you'd miss.

Absolutely a great use case. Monitoring KPIs and surfacing anomalies is exactly what AI analytics excels at. The operator sets the metrics that matter; AI watches them constantly and alerts when something needs attention.

steps.question: What is cohort analysis and why is it important for growth?
options: It's a way to sort users alphabetically

Cohort analysis groups users by when they started or a key action they took, then tracks their behavior over time — revealing retention patterns, churn timing, and which acquisition channels bring the best long-term customers
It's another name for A/B testing
It only works for SaaS companies with millions of users
feedback: Exactly. Cohort analysis is one of the most powerful growth tools because it reveals TIME-BASED patterns. You see not just that churn is happening, but WHEN it happens and WHICH groups of users are affected. This is where AI excels — finding cohort patterns across multiple dimensions simultaneously.

Close but that's simplified. Cohort analysis groups users by shared characteristics (usually signup date or first action) and tracks their behavior over time. AI supercharges this by automatically segmenting cohorts across dozens of dimensions.

steps.question: AI-powered A/B testing analysis can determine statistical significance faster than manual methods, but the operator still needs to define what to test and what metrics determine a winner.
feedback: Correct. AI speeds up the analysis — but the operator defines the experiment. What's the hypothesis? What metric matters? How long should it run? AI handles the math; you handle the strategy.

That's right. AI makes A/B testing faster and more rigorous, but it doesn't replace experimental design. The operator decides what to test, sets the success criteria, and interprets results in business context.

fields.label: What type of business or project are you analyzing?
fields.placeholder: e.g., SaaS app, e-commerce store, content site, local service business

fields.label: What are your key growth metrics?
fields.placeholder: e.g., monthly revenue, customer acquisition cost, retention rate, conversion rate, LTV

fields.label: Where does your growth data live?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Google Analytics, Stripe, Shopify, CRM, email platform, spreadsheets

fields.label: What growth questions do you most need answered?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Why is churn increasing? Which channel has best LTV? What's our fastest path to $100K MRR?

steps.body:
FUNNEL OPTIMIZATION PROMPT

"You are a conversion rate optimization specialist. I'm going to share my funnel data and I need you to:

1. Map every step in my conversion funnel with exact conversion rates between each step
2. Identify the single biggest drop-off point
3. Calculate the revenue impact of fixing that drop-off (what would X% improvement yield?)
4. Recommend 3 specific A/B test ideas to improve that stage — each with a clear hypothesis, the variable to change, the expected impact, and the success metric
5. Suggest how long each test should run to reach statistical significance

FUNNEL DATA: [paste your funnel stages with visitor counts or conversion numbers at each step]

Focus on actionable recommendations. Don't tell me what's wrong — tell me exactly what to test and in what order."

Use the funnel optimization prompt with a real or simulated funnel from your business (or make one up). Paste the AI's analysis below. Then identify the ONE recommendation you'd act on first — and say why.

steps.question: Your AI growth analytics system flags that your email acquisition channel's conversion rate dropped 30% this week. No other channels changed. What's your FIRST step?
options: Cut the email channel immediately — it's clearly broken
Ask the AI to dig deeper: compare email sends by segment, subject line performance, time of day, and recent list changes to find the specific cause
Ignore it — one week doesn't make a trend
Switch all budget to the best-performing channel
feedback: Exactly right. A 30% drop is a signal that needs investigation — not a trigger for rash action. AI analytics can drill into the data across multiple dimensions simultaneously and pinpoint the exact cause. Investigate before acting.
Cutting a channel after one week is premature. Use AI to dig deeper — compare by segment, subject line, timing, and recent list hygiene changes. The specific cause will determine the right response.

steps.prompt:
You've learned how AI transforms growth analytics — from descriptive reporting to predictive forecasting. Now think about how this applies to your situation.

Reflect on these questions and answer honestly.

questions.label: How do you currently approach growth analytics?
options: I don't track metrics at all — I'm flying blind
I look at basic metrics but don't do deep analysis
I analyze data regularly but it takes a lot of time
I have a decent analytics setup but want AI to find insights I'm missing

questions.label: What's the ONE growth metric that matters most to your business or project right now — and what would it mean if AI could help you improve it?
questions.placeholder: Describe the metric and the potential impact...

steps.title: Day 13 Complete! 📈
steps.body:
You now understand how AI transforms growth analytics — from data gathering to insight generation to automated action.

Key takeaways:
• AI analytics covers four levels: descriptive → diagnostic → predictive → prescriptive
• AI finds patterns and correlations across data sources at a speed humans can't match
• Funnel optimization with AI finds the exact drop-off points and recommends what to test
• Cohort analysis reveals time-based patterns that aggregate metrics hide
• Predictive analytics helps you see and act on trends before they fully materialize
• AI makes growth analytics accessible at any scale

Tomorrow: The Building Phase Capstone — AI for Sales & Marketing. You'll bring together everything you've learned to build a complete AI-powered sales and marketing system.
